# VantaUI CSS — Complete Reference

> A dark, angular, military-telemetry **tactical HUD** CSS library. Semantic-first like BeerCSS: write proper, plain semantic HTML and get a tactical UI — no component classes. Reach for one short helper word only to deviate. Responsive by default, OKLCH colors, zero runtime dependencies, framework-agnostic (Vue / Nuxt / vanilla). Optional tiny JS adds tabs, animated meters, drawers, dropdowns, toasts, tooltips, a live clock.

This is the COMPLETE single-file reference — reading it in full is enough to use the library correctly, with no link-following. For a lean index that links to focused topic files (cheaper to read selectively), see https://vantaui-css.khodok.com/llms.txt. Authoritative as of v1.5.2.

## Critical facts (read first)

- **The package name is `vantaui-css`.** It is NOT published to npm or any CDN yet — install it directly from GitHub (`github:Khoding/vantaui-css#<tag>`). All import specifiers start with `vantaui-css`.
- **You opt in with one class: `class="vui"`** on a root element (usually `<body>`). Inside `.vui`, plain semantic HTML is styled automatically. Outside `.vui`, only design tokens are defined — nothing global changes.
- **Semantic-first, almost zero classes.** A `<header>` containing a `<nav>` is an app bar. An `<article>` is a panel. A `<dialog class="left">` is a drawer. A `<meter>` is a telemetry bar. You rarely add classes; when you do, it is one short helper word (`glow`, `danger`, `small`, `left`).
- **All VantaUI styles live in cascade layers and are authored at zero specificity (`:where()`).** Your app's own (unlayered) classes ALWAYS win. You never need `!important` to override VantaUI.
- **`<i>text</i>` is an ICON** (Material Symbols ligature), e.g. `<i>home</i>` draws the home glyph. Use `<em>` for italic text, never `<i>`.
- Two prefixes exist: bare semantic styling needs **no prefix**; free-floating layout & utility helpers use the **`vui-`** prefix (collision-safe), e.g. `vui-grid`, `vui-p-4`, `vui-text-cyan`.
- CSS is fully usable with **no JavaScript**. JS only adds interactive niceties.

## Install

Not on npm/CDN yet — install from GitHub by tag. This adds a dependency that resolves like `"vantaui-css": "github:Khoding/vantaui-css#v1.5.2"`:

```bash
npm i github:Khoding/vantaui-css#v1.5.2
```

```js
import 'vantaui-css';                     // the stylesheet (package "main"/"style")
import { init } from 'vantaui-css/js';    // optional behaviours
init();                                   // boots tabs, animated meters, drawers, menus, toolbars, tooltips, clock
```

```html
<!-- Optional faster icon path (also bundled via @import in the CSS): -->
<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Material+Symbols+Outlined:opsz,wght,FILL,GRAD@24,400,0,0&display=block" />
<body class="vui">…</body>
```

Fonts (Chakra Petch · Rajdhani · Share Tech Mono · Material Symbols) load automatically via `@import` inside the stylesheet. Note: the remote `@import` breaks some processors (e.g. LightningCSS in a host build) — if so, vendor a copy with the font `@import` stripped and load the fonts separately.

Package entry points (`exports`): `vantaui-css` (min css), `vantaui-css/css` (unminified), `vantaui-css/min`, `vantaui-css/js`, `vantaui-css/js/global`, `vantaui-css/vue`, `vantaui-css/nuxt`, `vantaui-css/src/*`.

## Mental model — Settings · Elements · Helpers (the BeerCSS way)

- **Settings** = design tokens (CSS custom properties). Override any, anywhere — they cascade.
- **Elements** = semantic HTML styled directly inside `.vui`. No component classes.
- **Helpers** = one short element-scoped word to change a default (e.g. `<button class="danger">`).

State is semantic first: current nav item = `aria-current="page"` (or alias `class="active"`); invalid field = `aria-invalid`; a switch = `role="switch"`; alerts = `role="status"` / `role="alert"`; tabs = `role="tablist"`/`role="tab"`/`role="tabpanel"`.

### Minimal example

```html
<body class="vui">
  <header>
    <a href="/">VANTA<b>UI</b></a>
    <nav><a aria-current="page">Overview</a><a>Units</a></nav>
    <menu><button class="icon"><i>settings</i></button></menu>
  </header>
  <main>
    <h1>Command Center</h1>
    <article>
      <header><small class="vui-eyebrow">Diagnostics</small></header>
      <p>A chamfered panel. The header drew its own divider.</p>
      <footer><button type="submit">Authorize</button></footer>
    </article>
    <button>Override</button>
    <button class="danger">Abort</button>
    <label><input type="checkbox" role="switch" checked /> Active camo</label>
    <meter min="0" max="100" low="30" high="70" optimum="100" value="86"></meter>
  </main>
</body>
```

### What bare HTML gives you (inside `.vui`, no classes)

| Write | You get |
| --- | --- |
| `<h1>`…`<h6>` | Uppercase, tracked display headings |
| `<button>` | Chamfered outline button |
| `<button type="submit">` | Filled primary button |
| `<button class="icon"><i>close</i></button>` | Square icon button |
| `<i>home</i>` | The "home" Material Symbols glyph |
| `<a> <code> <kbd> <mark> <pre> <blockquote> <hr>` | Tactical inline + block styles |
| `<article>` | Chamfered panel; its `<header>`/`<footer>` auto-divide |
| `<input> <textarea> <select>` | Inset terminal fields with focus glow |
| `<input type="checkbox\|radio">` | Notched / round emissive marks |
| `<input type="checkbox" role="switch">` | Angular toggle switch |
| `<input type="range">` | Tactical slider |
| `<meter>` / `<progress>` | Telemetry bars (`<meter>` colors by `low`/`high`/`optimum`) |
| `<table>` | Mono data grid, eyebrow headers, cyan row hover |
| `<dl>` | Label │ value telemetry readout |
| `<details><summary>` | Zero-JS chamfered disclosure |
| `<dialog>` | Chamfered modal with scrim backdrop |
| `<header>` containing a `<nav>` | App bar; collapses to a burger when narrow |
| `<footer>` | Footer bar (`status` / `columns` shapes) |
| `<nav><ol>…</ol></nav>` | Breadcrumb (auto-detected, zero classes) |
| `.vui` element directly holding a `<main>` | Full app frame (landmarks placed by element) |
| `[role="status"]` / `[role="alert"]` | Info / threat alert with accent rail + glyph |
| `[role="tablist"]` / `[role="tab"]` | Tab strip (state via the JS helper or your framework) |

---

# Layout & Utilities

Free-floating helpers here use the `vui-` prefix.

## Breakpoints (mobile-first, min-width)

**s** 36rem/576px · **m** 48rem/768px · **l** 64rem/1024px · **xl** 80rem/1280px. Tokens: `--bp-{s,m,l,xl}`.

## Container

- `vui-container` — centered, max-width `--container-max`, inline padding `--container-pad`.
- `vui-container--narrow` — max-width `--container-narrow`. · `vui-container--fluid` — no max-width.

## Named flex composites (prefer these)

`vui-row` (+ `vui-row--wrap`) · `vui-col` · `vui-stack` (vertical rhythm, gap `--space-5`) · `vui-cluster` (wrapping horizontal) · `vui-center` (grid place-items center) · `vui-between` (space-between row) · `vui-spacer` (`flex:1 1 auto`).

## Auto grid

`vui-autogrid` (+ `vui-autogrid--fit`); control min track with `style="--vui-min: 16rem"`.

## 12-column grid

`vui-grid` wrapping children with span classes `vui-s1…vui-s12`, `vui-m1…vui-m12`, `vui-l1…vui-l12`. Children stack full-width on mobile, opt into spans at each breakpoint up.

```html
<div class="vui-grid">
  <article class="vui-s12 vui-m6 vui-l4">…</article>
  <article class="vui-s12 vui-m6 vui-l8">…</article>
</div>
<div class="vui-autogrid" style="--vui-min: 16rem">… cards …</div>
```

## Show / hide (hide-only, preserves natural display)

`vui-until-{s,m,l,xl}` (hide at/above the bp) · `vui-from-{s,m,l,xl}` (hide below the bp).

## Utility classes (all `vui-` prefixed)

Spacing index N → `--space-N`. Scale: 0=0, 1=2px, 2=4px, 3=8px, 4=12px, 5=16px, 6=20px, 7=24px, 8=32px (utilities cover 0–8; tokens go to 13).

- **Display**: `vui-block` `vui-inline-block` `vui-inline` `vui-flex` `vui-inline-flex` `vui-grid-d` `vui-contents` `vui-hidden` `vui-sr-only`.
- **Flex/grid behaviour**: `vui-flex-row` `vui-flex-col` `vui-wrap` `vui-nowrap` `vui-flex-1` `vui-grow` `vui-shrink-0`; `vui-items-{start,center,end,stretch,baseline}`; `vui-justify-{start,center,end,between,around}`.
- **Position/sizing**: `vui-relative` `vui-absolute` `vui-sticky` `vui-w-full` `vui-h-full` `vui-w-auto` `vui-min-w-0` `vui-overflow-auto` `vui-overflow-hidden`.
- **Gap**: `vui-gap-0…vui-gap-8`.
- **Padding**: `vui-p-0…8` (all), `vui-pi-{0,2..8}` (inline), `vui-pb-{0,2..8}` (block).
- **Margin**: `vui-m-{0,2,3,4,5,6}` `vui-m-auto` `vui-mi-auto`; `vui-mb-{0,2..8}` (block); `vui-mbe-{3,4,5,6}` (block-end only).
- **Text**: `vui-text-{start,center,end}` `vui-upper` `vui-nowrap-text` `vui-truncate`.
- **Font family**: `vui-font-{display,ui,hud,mono}`. **Weight**: `vui-fw-{light,regular,medium,semibold,bold}`. **Tracking**: `vui-tracked` `vui-tracked-wider` `vui-tracked-widest`. **Size**: `vui-text-{2xs,xs,sm,base,md,lg,xl,2xl,3xl,4xl,5xl}`.
- **Text color**: `vui-text-{primary,secondary,muted,faint,cyan,amber,red,green}`.
- **Surfaces**: `vui-bg-{void,base,raised,panel,inset}`.
- **Borders**: `vui-border` `vui-border-strong` `vui-border-accent` `vui-border-0`.
- **Glows**: `vui-glow-{cyan,amber,red,green}` `vui-text-glow`.
- **Misc**: `vui-pointer` `vui-select-none` `vui-flicker` `vui-scanning`.

## HUD primitives (base building blocks, `vui-` prefixed)

- **Type roles**: `vui-display` `vui-title` `vui-heading` `vui-mono` `vui-readout` (big numeric) `vui-label` `vui-eyebrow` (renders a `//` prefix) `vui-signal` (glowing cyan text).
- **Plates / frames**: `vui-chamfer` (cut top-left + bottom-right), `vui-notch` (all four corners cut), `vui-brackets` (L-shaped corner ticks), `vui-rule` (hairline with fading center).
- **Textured backdrops**: `vui-bg-grid` `vui-bg-scan` `vui-bg-hud` (ink + grid + scanlines + vignette).
- **Status dot**: `vui-dot` (default online/green, pulsing) + `vui-dot--warn` `vui-dot--threat` `vui-dot--cyan` `vui-dot--idle`.
- **Labelled divider**: `<div class="divider" data-label="SECTOR">` (label between fading hairlines; drop `data-label` for a plain divider).

## Prose layout (`vui-prose`) — long-form articles

A grid that centers text to a readable measure (`--prose-width`, 65ch) while letting assets bleed wider (`--bleed-width`, 85ch). Bleed wider: `vui-bleed`/`bleed`/`half-bleed` (or just `<img>`/`<figure>`/`<blockquote>`/`<pre>`, which bleed automatically). Edge-to-edge: `vui-bleed-full`/`bleed-full`. Apply to an `<article>` — the prose class opts that article out of panel styling.

---

# Navigation & Chrome

State is semantic-first: current item = `aria-current="page"` (alias `.active`); a leading `<i>` is its icon.

## App frame ("write the landmarks, get the layout")

Any `.vui` element that DIRECTLY holds a `<main>` becomes an app frame and places landmarks by element — no wrapper classes. On `<body>` it fills the viewport (`100dvh`); nested in a sized box it fills the box.

```html
<body class="vui">
  <header>…</header>            <!-- top bar -->
  <nav class="left">…</nav>      <!-- side rail: vertical ≥768px, bottom bar below -->
  <main>…</main>                <!-- the scrolling stage -->
  <footer>…</footer>            <!-- bottom bar (or use <nav class="bottom">) -->
</body>
```

- A bare `<aside>` works as a left rail; `<aside class="right">` / `<nav class="right">` is a right rail. Both rails at once are supported.
- A drawer sidebar (`<nav class="left drawer">` / `<aside class="drawer">`) sizes the rail column to `--drawer-width` (default 16.5rem).
- `header.inset` flips the default: rail(s) rise full-height, the header is confined beside them.
- The stage shows a faint hex-grid backdrop by default. Opt into vignette + scanlines with `.vignette` on the frame or `<body>` (off by default).
- Overflow philosophy: `<main>` is the scroll container; `min-inline-size: 0` confines wide children so chrome never slides. Genuine misuse scrolls visibly rather than being silently clipped.

## Header / app bar

A `<header>` becomes a tactical app bar when it carries a `<nav>`, is the top bar of an app frame, or sits directly on a `.vui` `<body>`.

```html
<header>
  <a href="/">VANTA<b>UI</b></a>           <!-- brand: first link/heading; <b> is accent-lit; an <img>/<svg> is the emblem -->
  <nav>                                     <!-- inline primary nav; first <nav> pushes everything after it right -->
    <a aria-current="page">Overview</a>
    <a>Units <small>12</small></a>          <!-- trailing <small> = count chip -->
  </nav>
  <form><i>search</i><input type="search" placeholder="Search…"></form>  <!-- inline search -->
  <menu><button class="icon"><i>settings</i></button></menu>             <!-- trailing actions -->
  <button class="icon" aria-controls="drawer" data-open="drawer"><i>menu</i></button>  <!-- burger -->
</header>
```

The header is a **container-query context**: when the BAR itself narrows below 52rem, the inline nav + search collapse and the menu button (any header `<button>` with `aria-controls`/`commandfor`/`command`/`data-open`) appears. Inside it: an `<hr>` is a vertical divider; a `<menu>` is the trailing actions cluster. Shape/tone helpers: `sticky` `glow` `bare` `tall` `center` `float`. Alignment: `max` · `right` / `left`.

## Footer

- **default** — hairline strip: brand · a `<nav>` of links · `<small>` legal line. Alignment `max` / `right`.
- **`footer.status`** — dense HUD telemetry bar; each direct child is a readout cell. `.signal` lights a cell, `.max` pushes cells apart, a `<b>` is the value.
- **`footer.columns`** — sitemap; columns are `<nav>`/`<section>`/`<div>` with a heading (`h4`–`h6`/`.label`) + links. A child `.bottom` spans full width.
- Tone: `glow` · `base`.

## Navigation rails, bottom bar, drawers

- **Side rail**: `<nav class="left">` / `<nav class="right">` / a bare `<aside>`. Bottom bar on phones, vertical column from `m` up. A leading `<img>`/`<svg>` is an emblem; `.max` a spacer.
- **Bottom command bar**: `<nav class="bottom">`; pin a standalone one with `nav.bottom.fixed`.
- **Off-canvas drawer**: `<dialog class="left">` / `<dialog class="right">` — native `<dialog>`, animated, opened via `showModal()` (JS `drawers()` wires `[data-open]`/`[data-close]`/backdrop dismiss). Structure: `> header`, `> nav` (link stack), `> footer`.
- **Persistent drawer sidebar**: `<aside class="drawer">` / `<nav class="drawer">` — full-height column on desktop, off-canvas (`.active`/`.open`) on mobile. `.right` flips the side; width via `--drawer-width`.

```html
<dialog class="left" id="drawer">
  <header><a href="/"><img src="mark.svg" alt=""> VANTAUI</a><button class="icon" data-close><i>close</i></button></header>
  <nav>
    <h6>Sectors</h6>
    <a aria-current="page"><i>dashboard</i> Overview</a>
    <a><i>group</i> Units</a>
  </nav>
  <footer><button class="block" data-close>Close</button></footer>
</dialog>
```

## Breadcrumb (detected, zero classes)

A `<nav>` directly containing an `<ol>` (or `ol.breadcrumb`). `/` separators inserted automatically; mark the last item `aria-current="page"`.

```html
<nav><ol><li><a>Home</a></li><li><a>Units</a></li><li aria-current="page">Alpha</li></ol></nav>
```

## Dropdown / action menu

- **`<details class="dropdown">`** — zero-JS, everywhere. `<summary>` is the trigger; the panel is a `<menu>`/`<nav>`/`<ul>`. JS `menus()` adds outside-click/Esc dismissal and upgrades to the Popover top layer where supported.
- **`<button popovertarget>` + `[popover].menu`** — modern top-layer + light-dismiss path.

Items are `<a>`/`<button>` (lead with an `<i>`); an `<hr>` divides, an `<h6>`/`.label` is a group heading, `.danger` tones an item red. Add `.right` to align the panel to the trailing edge. A trailing `<i>` in the summary is a chevron that rotates open.

```html
<details class="dropdown">
  <summary>Actions <i>expand_more</i></summary>
  <menu>
    <h6>Unit</h6>
    <button><i>edit</i> Rename</button>
    <hr>
    <button class="danger"><i>delete</i> Decommission</button>
  </menu>
</details>
```

## Tabs

A `[role="tablist"]` of `[role="tab"]` buttons; active tab read from `aria-selected="true"` (or `.active`). Panels are `[role="tabpanel"]`, toggled with `[hidden]`. A trailing `<small>` is a count chip. Wire with the JS `tabs()` helper or your framework.

```html
<div role="tablist">
  <button role="tab" aria-selected="true" aria-controls="p1">Sensors</button>
  <button role="tab" aria-controls="p2">Comms <small>3</small></button>
</div>
<div role="tabpanel" id="p1">…</div>
<div role="tabpanel" id="p2" hidden>…</div>
```

## Pagination

`<nav class="pagination">` wrapping a `<ul>`/`<ol>` of page links (`<a>`/`<button>`; a lone `<i>` is an icon prev/next). Mark the current page `aria-current="page"`, unavailable controls `disabled`/`aria-disabled`. A bare `<span>` is an ellipsis gap. `.compact` tightens it.

## Toolbar

A `<div role="toolbar">` (or `.toolbar`) is a chamfered control track holding buttons, button groups (`<menu class="group">`), segmented controls, an `<hr>` separator, a `.max` spacer, and short `<span>`/`<small>` labels. JS `toolbars()` gives it roving-tabindex keyboard nav. `.vertical` stands it on end.

---

# Forms & Buttons

## Buttons

A bare `<button>` is a chamfered outline control; `type="submit"` auto-promotes to filled primary. Style other elements as a button with `.button`. Tune with one word:

- **Variant**: `fill` · `ghost`. **Tones**: `amber` · `danger` (red) · `secure` (green); default cyan. **Sizes**: `small` · `large`. **Width**: `block`.
- **Icon-only**: `.icon` → 38px square (also `icon small` 30px, `icon large` 46px). Icon + text just works without `.icon`. `aria-pressed="true"` lights an icon toggle.

```html
<button>Override</button>
<button type="submit">Authorize</button>
<button class="danger">Abort</button>
<button><i>save</i> Save</button>
<button class="icon"><i>close</i></button>
<a class="button ghost" href="/docs">Docs</a>
```

**Button group**: `<menu class="group">` (or any `.group`) is an inline-flex cluster of buttons with consistent gaps.

## Text fields, textarea, select

`<input>` (text/email/password/number/search/url/…), `<textarea>`, `<select>` become inset terminal fields (mono, gentle chamfer, focus glow), full-width in their container. `<textarea>` resizes vertically; `<select>` gets a cyan chevron. Focus → accent border + glow. Validation: `aria-invalid="true"` → red border + glow.

## Field wrapper

A `<label>` wrapping a text control stacks: leading `<span>` = uppercase label, control below, trailing `<small>` = hint (`<small class="error">` is red). A `<label>` wrapping a checkbox/radio lays out as an inline row.

```html
<label>
  <span>Callsign</span>
  <input type="text" placeholder="e.g. RAVEN-1" />
  <small>Letters, numbers, dash.</small>
</label>
<label><input type="checkbox" /> Enable telemetry</label>
```

## Checkbox, radio, switch, range

- `<input type="checkbox">` notched mark; `<input type="radio">` round mark — drawn on the element, light cyan when checked.
- `<input type="checkbox" role="switch">` — angular toggle.
- `<input type="range">` — tactical slider with a chamfered cyan thumb.

```html
<label><input type="checkbox" role="switch" checked /> Active camo</label>
<label><span>Output</span><input type="range" min="0" max="100" value="72" /></label>
```

Any native control not fully restyled inherits `accent-color: var(--accent)`.

---

# Data Display

## Panel (`<article>`)

A bare `<article>` is a chamfered plate; an inner `<header>`/`<footer>` auto-divides. Depth: `raised` · `inset` · `flat`. Shape: `notch` · `brackets`. Glow: `glow`. Color tint: `cyan` · `amber` · `red` · `green`. Tint intensity: `dim` · `vivid`. Combine freely (`"amber glow"`, `"green raised"`).

```html
<article class="raised">
  <header><small class="vui-eyebrow">Diagnostics</small> <small>v2.1</small></header>
  <p>A chamfered panel; the header drew its own divider.</p>
  <footer><button type="submit">Authorize</button></footer>
</article>
```

(`<article class="vui-prose">` opts out of panel styling into the prose layout.)

## Table

A bare `<table>` is a mono data grid (eyebrow headers, cyan row hover). Use real `<thead>`/`<tbody>`/`<th>`/`<td>`; wide tables self-scroll.

## Description list, key/value, stat

- `<dl>` — label │ value telemetry grid (`<dt>` label, `<dd>` mono value); `.stacked` for label-over-value.
- `.kv` — a single key/value row (label left, mono value right).
- `.stat` — big numeric readout: `<b>` the number, `<span>` the caption; `.stat.signal` / `.stat.amber` recolor.

```html
<dl><dt>Callsign</dt><dd>RAVEN-1</dd><dt>Sector</dt><dd>NE-04</dd></dl>
<div class="stat signal"><b>98.6</b><span>Integrity</span></div>
```

## Badge

`.badge` chip (on `<span>`/`<a>`/`<mark>`). Tone: `cyan` · `amber` · `red` · `green` · `neutral`. `.solid` fills, `.dot` adds a live dot. Long labels truncate.

```html
<span class="badge green dot">Online</span>
<span class="badge red solid">Threat</span>
```

## Avatar

Initials on `<span>`/`<div>` or an `<img>`. Shape: `round` · `hex`. Size: `small` · `large`. Status frame: `online` · `busy` · `idle` · `offline`. `.avatar-group` overlaps several.

```html
<span class="avatar online">R1</span>
<img class="avatar round large" src="op.jpg" alt="Raven-1" />
<div class="avatar-group"><span class="avatar">R1</span><span class="avatar">B2</span></div>
```

## Timeline

`<ol class="timeline">` — vertical event feed, each `<li>` a glowing node on a rail. Lead with a `<time>` stamp; tone a node with a color word on the `<li>`. `.log` for a dense mono feed.

```html
<ol class="timeline">
  <li class="green"><time>09:42:01</time> Uplink established.</li>
  <li class="red"><time>09:45:00</time> Link lost.</li>
</ol>
```

## Stepper

`<ol class="stepper">` — numbered steps + connector. Active step `aria-current="step"`; `.done` clears a step (check replaces the number). `.vertical` for vertical layout. Static — wire navigation yourself.

```html
<ol class="stepper">
  <li class="done">Authenticate</li>
  <li aria-current="step">Configure</li>
  <li>Deploy</li>
</ol>
```

## Tree

`<ul class="tree">` — hierarchy with guide rails. Branch: `<li><details><summary>…</summary><ul>…</ul></details></li>` (zero-JS collapse). Leaf: `<li><a>`/`<li><span>`. Lead rows with an `<i>`; selected leaf `aria-current="page"`. For a full ARIA tree widget add `role="tree"`/`treeitem`/`group` + `aria-expanded` and drive focus in JS.

```html
<ul class="tree">
  <li><details open>
    <summary><i>folder</i> Sector NE</summary>
    <ul><li><a aria-current="page"><i>description</i> Brief.md</a></li></ul>
  </details></li>
</ul>
```

## Carousel

`<div class="carousel">` — horizontal scroll-snap track; direct children are slides. Swipe/scroll/keyboard everywhere; native arrows + dots on CSS-carousel browsers. Size slides with `--vui-slide`. `.peek` · `.full` · `.clean` · `.no-arrows` · `.no-dots`.

```html
<div class="carousel peek" style="--vui-slide: 18rem">
  <article>Slide 1</article><article>Slide 2</article>
</div>
```

## Rating

`<fieldset class="rating">` — a row of pips (each a `<label>` with a leading `<i>`, immediately followed by its radio `<input>`, authored 1…5). `<legend>` is the group label. Tone with a color word; `.readonly` for a static readout. Zero-JS.

## Segmented control

`.segmented` track of `<label>` chips, each wrapping a radio `<input>` (+ optional leading `<i>`). The checked segment lights. `.small`, plus color tones. Zero-JS radiogroup.

```html
<div class="segmented">
  <label><input type="radio" name="view" checked /> Map</label>
  <label><input type="radio" name="view" /> List</label>
</div>
```

---

# Feedback & Status

## Alerts (inline)

`[role="status"]` = info (cyan); `[role="alert"]` = threat (red). Accent rail + leading glyph; an optional `<strong>`/`<h*>` is the title. Re-tone: `info` · `warn` · `threat` · `secure`. Override the glyph with `--vui-alert-glyph`.

```html
<div role="status"><strong>Online</strong> Telemetry uplink restored.</div>
<div role="alert"><strong>Threat</strong> Hostile contact, sector NE-04.</div>
```

## Toast (transient)

Raise imperatively with the JS `toast()` helper (owns an `aria-live` `.toaster` region, reuses the alert rail + glyph). Tone: `info` · `warn` · `threat` · `secure`. Place the region with `.start`/`.top`/`.center`.

```js
import { toast } from 'vantaui-css/js';
toast('Telemetry uplink restored', { tone: 'secure', title: 'ONLINE' });
```

## Meters & progress

- Native `<progress>` — cyan bar. Native `<meter>` — colors by `low`/`high`/`optimum` thresholds.
- `.meter` helper — labelled accent bar, no JS: a `<span>`/`<label>` label + a `<b>` value (leave empty → renders `--value%`). Tone `cyan`/`amber`/`red`/`green`; `.segmented` for ticks. Set `--value` (and `--max`).

```html
<meter min="0" max="100" low="30" high="70" optimum="100" value="86"></meter>
<div class="meter amber" style="--value:72"><span>Power</span><b></b></div>
```

## Radial gauge

`.gauge` — a 270° arc in pure CSS driven by `--value`. Structure: `<b>` (number, empty → counter), optional `<small>` (unit), `<span>` (caption). Size `small`/`large`; tone color words.

```html
<div class="gauge" style="--value:72"><b></b><small>%</small><span>Integrity</span></div>
```

## Spinner (indeterminate)

`.spinner` — a sweeping ring on a `<span>`/`<div>`. Tone color words, size `small`/`large`. A trailing text node is an inline label; `.block` centers it. Mark `role="status"` + `aria-label`.

```html
<span class="spinner" role="status" aria-label="Loading"></span>
<span class="spinner cyan">Syncing…</span>
```

## Skeleton

`.skeleton` — a dim scanning placeholder. Bare = block, `.text` = type line (add `.last` for a short final line), `.circle` = disc, `.block` = a tall block. Mark the group `aria-hidden="true"`.

## Empty state

`.empty` — centered placeholder: a leading `<i>` glyph, a heading, a `<p>`, and a trailing `<menu>`/`.actions`.

```html
<div class="empty">
  <i>satellite_alt</i><h3>No active units</h3>
  <p>Deploy a unit to begin receiving telemetry.</p>
  <menu><button type="submit">Deploy unit</button></menu>
</div>
```

## Live status dot

`vui-dot` (pulsing online/green) + `vui-dot--warn` · `vui-dot--threat` · `vui-dot--cyan` · `vui-dot--idle`.

---

# Theming & Design Tokens

Tokens live on `:root`, available everywhere (outside `.vui` ONLY tokens are defined). Override any, anywhere — they cascade. The single signal accent is electric cyan (`--accent`).

```css
:root {
  --accent: oklch(84.6% 0.133 212.1);
  --bevel-md: 14px;
  --container-max: 90rem;
}
```

## Colors (OKLCH)

Every `src/` token ships twice (sRGB hex fallback, then `oklch()`). Palette meaning: cyan = you/system, amber = caution/hostile, red = threat, green = secure/online.

- **Scales**: `--ink-{1000,900,800,700,600,500,400}`, `--cyan-{50,200,400,500,600,700}`+`--cyan-glow`, `--amber-{300,400,500,600}`+`--amber-glow`, `--red-{400,500,600}`+`--red-glow`, `--green-{400,500,600}`+`--green-glow`, `--slate-{50,200,300,400,500,600}`.
- **Aliases**: `--bg-{void,base,raised}`, `--surface-{panel,panel-2,inset}`, `--text-{primary,secondary,muted,faint,signal,invert}`, `--accent` `--accent-strong` `--accent-dim` `--accent-glow`, `--status-{online,warn,threat,hostile}`, `--hairline` `--hairline-strong` `--hairline-accent` `--divider` `--scrim`.

## Spacing & geometry

`--space-0…--space-13`, `--radius-{none,xs,sm,pill}`, `--bevel-{sm,md,lg,xl}`, `--clip-chamfer` / `--clip-notch`, `--border-{hair,thin,bold}`, `--container-max` `--container-narrow` `--container-pad` `--gutter` `--hud-edge` `--drawer-width`, `--bp-{s,m,l,xl}`.

## Typography

`--font-{display,ui,hud,mono,icon}`, `--fw-{light,regular,medium,semibold,bold}`, fluid `--fs-{2xs,xs,sm,base,md,lg,xl,2xl,3xl,4xl,5xl}` (base+ use `clamp()`), `--lh-{tight,snug,normal,mono}`, `--ls-{tight,normal,wide,wider,widest}`, semantic `--text-{display,title,heading,body,label,mono}-size`.

## Effects & motion

`--glow-cyan-{sm,md,lg}` `--glow-{amber,red,green}-md`, `--text-glow-{cyan,amber}`, `--shadow-{panel,raised,inset}`, textures `--scanlines` `--grid-fine`(+`--grid-fine-size`) `--vignette`, motion `--ease-{hud,snap,inout}` `--dur-{fast,base,slow,boot}`, `--focus-ring`.

Keyframes: `vui-flicker`, `vui-scan`, `vui-pulse`, `vui-sweep`. A global `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` rule neutralizes ambient animation.

---

# Optional JavaScript (`vantaui-css/js`)

ES module exports (also attached to `window.vui` as a plain script; auto-inits on `DOMContentLoaded`). Every function takes an optional `root` (default `document`) and is idempotent.

- `init(root = document)` — runs all behaviours below.
- `tabs(root)` — wires `[role="tablist"]`/`[role="tab"]` (click + arrow keys); panels matched by `aria-controls`/`data-tab`; toggles `[hidden]`, sets `aria-selected`/`tabindex`.
- `setValue(el, n, duration = 600)` — animate a `.meter`/`.gauge` `--value` (ease-out cubic; respects reduced motion). Max from `--max`/`data-max` (default 100).
- Auto-animate (via `init`): elements with `data-value` + `data-animate` fill from 0 on scroll into view.
- `drawers(root)` — `[data-open="id"]` opens a `<dialog id>` (`showModal()`, or toggles `.active`/`.open`), `[data-close]`, backdrop dismiss (opt out `data-no-dismiss`), drawer-link auto-close.
- `menus(root)` — `<details class="dropdown">`; upgrades to the Popover top layer where available (light-dismiss, Esc, exclusive open), positions below the trigger (`.right` to right-align).
- `toolbars(root)` — roving tabindex for `[role="toolbar"]` (`.vertical` for up/down).
- `tooltips(root)` — shows `[data-tip="text"]` on hover/focus via a body-level fixed element (never clipped).
- Live clock (via `init`): writes `HH:MM:SS` into `[data-vui-clock]` every second.
- `toast(message, options?)` — snackbar. Options `{ tone, title, duration = 4000, role = 'status', dismissible = true, placement }`; `tone` accepts a bare string shorthand. Returns `{ el, dismiss }`.

```js
import { init, toast, setValue } from 'vantaui-css/js';
init();
toast('Telemetry uplink restored', { tone: 'secure', title: 'ONLINE' });
```

Default export: `VantaUI = { init, tabs, setValue, drawers, tooltips, menus, toolbars, toast }`. Entry points: `vantaui-css/js` (ESM), `vantaui-css/js/global` (plain-script global).

## Vue 3

```js
import { createApp } from 'vue';
import VantaUI from 'vantaui-css/vue'; // imports CSS, adds .vui to <body>, boots behaviours
import App from './App.vue';
createApp(App).use(VantaUI).mount('#app');
// options: app.use(VantaUI, { behaviours: false, bodyClass: false, root: el })
```

## Nuxt 3

```ts
// nuxt.config.ts
export default defineNuxtConfig({
  modules: ['vantaui-css/nuxt'],
  VantaUI: { behaviours: true, bodyClass: true }, // both default true
});
```

The module registers the stylesheet, adds `.vui` to `<body>` app-wide, and (client-side) boots the optional behaviours.

---

## Common mistakes to avoid

- Do NOT use `<i>` for italics — it renders an icon glyph. Use `<em>`.
- Do NOT add component classes like `.panel`, `.card`, `.btn` — use the semantic element. Helpers are single words, not BEM.
- Do NOT prefix bare semantic usage; the `vui-` prefix is only for free-floating layout/utility/primitive helpers.
- Do NOT use `!important` to override VantaUI — write an unlayered class; it already wins.
- The package is `vantaui-css` and installs from GitHub — it is not on npm or any CDN yet.
- The default `main` is the minified CSS; use `vantaui-css/css` for the unminified build.

## Browser support

Evergreen browsers from ~2023. Requires `:has()`, cascade layers, container queries, `color-mix()`, `oklch()`, `mask`, `conic-gradient`: Chrome/Edge 111+, Firefox 121+, Safari 16.4+. Progressive enhancements (animated `<details>`, native CSS carousel controls, anchor-positioned popovers) degrade gracefully on older engines.

MIT. An original tactical-HUD interpretation — no trademarked marks or character likenesses.
